open_opportunities.name: Innovate UK Smart Grant
open_opportunities.eligibility: UK-registered businesses. No PhD required.
open_opportunities.description: Supports disruptive innovations with commercial potential. Quarterly rounds.
open_opportunities.notes: R&A Consulting can apply directly. Priority: Aureon AI commercialization.

open_opportunities.name: National Space Innovation Programme
open_opportunities.eligibility: UK organisations. SMEs encouraged.
open_opportunities.description: Supports innovative space technology and services.
open_opportunities.notes: Perfect fit for LSSP and EPAS. Contact innovation team.

open_opportunities.name: AIRR Compute: AI Open Access
open_opportunities.eligibility: UK registered businesses and eligible UK research organisations can apply for AIRR AI Open Access compute.
open_opportunities.description: Compute-only access to AIRR resources including Isambard-AI for AI-driven research and scientific discovery.
open_opportunities.notes: Portal draft created for proposal 0261-8797-7601-1. Final submit requires subsidy, peer-review, and company filing checks.

open_opportunities.name: Wellcome Discovery Research
open_opportunities.eligibility: Must be at eligible UK/EU research organisation.
open_opportunities.description: Responsive-mode funding for discovery research.
open_opportunities.notes: Need university affiliation. Strong fit for PEFCφS.

open_opportunities.name: Leverhulme Trust Research Project Grants
open_opportunities.eligibility: UK universities. Academic staff.
open_opportunities.description: Supports original research in any discipline.
open_opportunities.notes: Need university affiliation. Fundamental physics fit.

open_opportunities.name: Horizon Europe EIC Accelerator
open_opportunities.eligibility: EU-based companies. UK companies can participate (associate status).
open_opportunities.description: Grants + equity for breakthrough innovations.
open_opportunities.notes: Apply through Innovate UK as UK national contact point.

open_opportunities.name: Templeton Foundation Science & Big Questions
open_opportunities.eligibility: Open. International researchers welcome.
open_opportunities.description: Supports research at the intersection of science and big questions (consciousness, meaning, etc.).
open_opportunities.notes: PERFECT FIT. Your HNC framework and consciousness research are exactly what they fund.

open_opportunities.name: Schmidt Sciences / Schmidt Futures
open_opportunities.eligibility: Open. Interdisciplinary research.
open_opportunities.description: Supports ambitious interdisciplinary research.
open_opportunities.notes: Your interdisciplinary AI + physics + neuroscience is exactly what they want.

open_opportunities.name: Simons Foundation Collaboration Grants
open_opportunities.eligibility: Open. Mathematics and physical sciences focus.
open_opportunities.description: Supports mathematics, physics, and life sciences.
open_opportunities.notes: HNC harmonic mathematics and physics are strong fit.

open_opportunities.name: ARPA-E Energy Research
open_opportunities.eligibility: US entities. International collaboration.
open_opportunities.description: Supports high-risk energy technology research.
open_opportunities.notes: LSSP space energy and Illumination Chip. PERFECT FIT.

open_opportunities.name: ESA Open Space Innovation Platform
open_opportunities.eligibility: ESA member states. UK is associate member.
open_opportunities.description: Supports innovative space technology ideas.
open_opportunities.notes: LSSP and EPAS for ESA missions. UK associate member.

open_opportunities.name: ESA Space Solutions - Business Applications
open_opportunities.eligibility: ESA member states. SMEs encouraged.
open_opportunities.description: Supports commercial applications of space technology.
open_opportunities.notes: LSSP commercial space energy. R&A Consulting as SME.

open_opportunities.name: NIH BRAIN Initiative
open_opportunities.eligibility: US institutions. International collaboration.
open_opportunities.description: Supports neuroscience and brain research.
open_opportunities.notes: PEFCφS neural coherence and consciousness.

open_opportunities.name: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative Science Grants
open_opportunities.eligibility: Open. International researchers welcome.
open_opportunities.description: Supports open science, neuroscience, and biomedical research.
open_opportunities.notes: Your open-science philosophy and PEFCφS research are strong fit.

open_opportunities.name: Patrick J. McGovern Foundation
open_opportunities.eligibility: Open. AI for good focus.
open_opportunities.description: Supports AI for social good and neuroscience.
open_opportunities.notes: Aureon AI + PEFCφS neuroscience = strong fit.

open_opportunities.name: Next Wave: Breakthrough Wave 1
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants or collaborations. To lead a collaborative project your organisation must be a UK registered micro, small or medium sized enterprise (SME) claiming grant funding on this application. To work alone your organisation must be a UK registered SME .
open_opportunities.description: UK registered businesses can apply for a share of up to £10 million for industrial research projects to explore industrial strategy aligned Createch innovation. This funding is from Innovate UK.
open_opportunities.notes: Live application 10209601 was submitted through the Innovation Funding Service. Do not reopen unless a deliberate resubmission decision is made. | IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Already submitted for the Aureon Evidence Studio route; monitor assessment and do not duplicate. Next action: Monitor submitted IFS application 10209601. Raw closes text: 11 August 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: UK-Switzerland CR&D Round 3
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to UK and Swiss collaborations only. To lead a project your organisation must be a UK registered: business of any size must be or involve at least one grant claiming UK registered micro, small or medium sized enterprise (SME) collaborate with a Swiss implementation partner and at least one research institute
open_opportunities.description: UK registered businesses can apply for a share of up to £3 million for innovative projects in specific technology areas. You must collaborate with at least one Swiss implementation partner applying under the equivalent Swiss Innosuisse programme.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Potential collaborative route; cannot be led alone because Swiss implementation and research partners are required. Next action: Create partner shortlist and collaboration abstract. Raw closes text: 3 September 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: Scaling performance of quantum computing hardware: CR&D
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to collaborations only. To lead a collaborative project your organisation must be a UK registered business of any size or a research and technology organisation (RTO).
open_opportunities.description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £33 million to develop device level hardware and associated software to meet the scale, programmability, and runtime performance needs of universal fault tolerant quantum computers.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Indirect fit through software/runtime assurance only; needs quantum hardware lead partner. Next action: Prepare partner approach note; do not submit standalone. Raw closes text: 18 September 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: DRIVE35 Innovation Fund: Collaborate 3
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to collaborations only.
open_opportunities.description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £20 million for late stage collaborative research and development projects. Projects will support the UK’s transition to zero emission vehicles and a pathway to a net zero automotive industry.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Possible only with transport/manufacturing partner. Current Aureon evidence is enabling software, not vehicle hardware. Next action: Log partner-required route; no standalone submission. Raw closes text: 16 September 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: CfI: Earlier identification of UK children with SEN
open_opportunities.eligibility: To lead a project, you can: be an organisation of any size, including those based in the EU, EEA or internationally work alone or with other organisations as subcontractors Contracts will be awarded to a single legal entity only.
open_opportunities.description: Organisations can apply for a share of £2.5m inclusive of VAT to develop innovations that enable earlier, fairer, more reliable identification of special educational needs (SEN) in contexts where existing market solutions underperform or do not operate.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Possible responsible-AI evidence tooling route; needs education/SEN partner and careful ethics framing. Next action: Build scope check and partner note. Raw closes text: 5 August 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: Future Leaders Fellowships: Round 11, business and non-academic
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants with supporting organisations. To host a Future Leaders Fellowship your UK-registered organisation must be a: business of any size charity catapult public body agri-tech centre eligible research organisation
open_opportunities.description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £110 million to deliver ambitious research and innovation and develop future leaders. This funding is from UK Research and Innovation (UKRI).
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: High-value next pack. Business/non-academic route can frame Gary/Aureon as a future leader if host/organisational eligibility is satisfied. Next action: Build leadership fellowship pack and partner/host evidence register. Raw closes text: 4 November 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: DRIVE35: Scale Up Fund
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants and collaborations. To lead a project your organisation must be a UK registered business of any size. Non-UK registered businesses may apply but must set up a UK registered business before any grant can be awarded. The UK registered business must deliver your project. You will not be able to start your project until the business is set up with UK staff in place.
open_opportunities.description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £150 million, to create scale up manufacturing facilities for zero emission vehicle technologies.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Possible only with transport/manufacturing partner. Current Aureon evidence is enabling software, not vehicle hardware. Next action: Log partner-required route; no standalone submission. Raw closes text: No submission deadline.

open_opportunities.name: Consumer Led Flexibility for the Clean Energy Superpower Mission
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants and collaborations. To work as a sole applicant or lead a collaborative project your organisation must be a UK registered business of any size, a research and technology organisation (RTO), charity, not for profit, public sector organisation, or academic institution.
open_opportunities.description: UK registered organisations can apply for a share of up to £25 million for pre-commercialisation projects. This funding is from the UKRI R&D Missions Accelerator Programme through the Clean Energy Superpower Mission.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Potential fit if Aureon is scoped as consumer-energy flexibility evidence, assurance, and orchestration tooling. Next action: Draft scope note and check energy-domain partner need. Raw closes text: 26 August 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: Innovation Loans Expression of Interest
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to single applications from UK registered micro, small or medium sized enterprises (SMEs) only.
open_opportunities.description: UK registered micro, small or medium sized enterprises can submit an Expression of Interest for loans for close to market innovative projects with strong commercial potential. They must significantly improve the UK economy and drive societal benefit.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Useful non-grant finance route, but loan risk requires a finance-readiness check before submission. Next action: Prepare EOI narrative; flag as loan, not grant. Raw closes text: No submission deadline.

open_opportunities.name: Full ADOPT Grant: Round 8
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to collaborations only.
open_opportunities.description: Farming, growing or forestry businesses based in England can apply for a share of up to £5 million for on-farm trial and demonstration projects, to improve adoption of new ideas or solutions in the agricultural sector.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Not a direct R&A/Aureon lead route. Only pursue if an agricultural partner brings the live trial. Next action: Monitor; no standalone application. Raw closes text: 29 July 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: ACTASAP Pilot Phase 1
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to both single applicants and collaborations. To work alone or lead a collaborative project your organisation must be a UK registered academic institution.
open_opportunities.description: UK registered academic institutions can apply for a share of up to £800,000 to take part in the ACTASAP. This funding is from the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) and delivered by Innovate UK, part of UKRI.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Academic institution lead required. Aureon can only support if invited by an eligible academic lead. Next action: Monitor or approach academic partners only. Raw closes text: 28 July 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: Accelerated Knowledge Transfer Partnerships 6 (AKT 6)
open_opportunities.eligibility: To lead a project your organisation must: be a UK registered higher education or further education institution, research and technology organisation (RTO) or Catapult collaborate with a UK registered business of any size with four or more full time employees The business must contribute a minimum of 10% to the project’s costs.
open_opportunities.description: UK registered academic institutions, RTOs or Catapults can apply for a share of up to £2.5 million to fund innovation projects with businesses.
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Aureon can be the business problem owner, but an academic/RTO/Catapult lead is required. Next action: Use as academic partnership target. Raw closes text: 15 July 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.

open_opportunities.name: Commercialising Knowledge Assets Fund (CKAF) Autumn
open_opportunities.eligibility: This competition is open to single applicants. Grants shall be awarded to, and administered by, the applicant organisation. To lead a project your organisation must be a UK registered: Public Sector Research Establishment (PRSE), UKRI wholly owned institute or other public sector bodies that meet GOTT standard eligibility criteria, for example, arms length bodies (ALBs), that undertake research as a primary function
open_opportunities.description: UK registered government research organisations can apply for grants from £50,000 up to £250,000 to move viable public sector knowledge assets towards commercial readiness. This funding is provided by the Government Office for Technology Transfer (GOTT).
open_opportunities.notes: IFS current sweep 2026-07-09. Route decision: Government research organisation route. Not a direct R&A lead route. Next action: Monitor; no standalone application. Raw closes text: 1 September 2026. IFS search result gave date only; 11:00 UK time is a tracker placeholder until the competition-specific page is checked.
